title: Plugin Privileges Contract (v1.0)
description: Declarative privilege contract for an evo plugin or framework surface. One file per surface (plugin / steward / distribution-bootstrap). Travels with the plugin through every admission path. The framework refuses to admit any surface whose declared contract diverges from host provisioning in either direction (under-provisioning OR over-provisioning).

schema_version.description: Schema major.minor. Breaking changes increment major; additive changes increment minor. Deprecation precedes removal by at least one minor release.

owner.description: Team / individual / organisation accountable for this surface's contract.

isolation.description: How this surface runs relative to the steward identity. 'oop' = its own systemd unit with isolated identity; 'in_process' = loaded into the steward (privileges union into steward identity, audited as the higher-trust class).

capability_intent.description: What the surface needs to do, in domain terms. Each entry is one independently-testable intent. The framework grants the OS-level privileges that satisfy each intent's access_path.

id.description: Stable identifier for this intent within the surface. lowercase_snake_case.

need.description: Human-readable description of what the surface needs to do.

access_path.description: Mechanism class through which the intent is satisfied. Examples: 'dbus/polkit', 'filesystem/state', 'network/outbound', 'alsa/control', 'gpio/sysfs', 'systemd/unit-control'.

failure_mode.description: What happens when permission is absent at runtime. MUST be non-panic and observable. Example: 'degraded status; no-panic; structured event emitted'.

required_binaries.description: External executables the surface invokes at runtime. The admission-time parity gate verifies presence + minimum version on the host.

name.description: Binary name as it appears on PATH (e.g. 'nmcli', 'mpd', 'ffmpeg'). Path-like values not permitted; use PATH lookup.

min_version.description: Minimum acceptable version, semver-shaped. Optional. The framework calls the binary's --version (or equivalent) and compares.

failure_mode.description: What happens when the binary is missing or below min_version. MUST be non-panic. Example: 'refuse to start; reason logged'.

required_kernel_modules.description: Kernel modules that must be loaded (or loadable) for the surface to function. Each entry is the module name as 'modprobe' / '/proc/modules' would report it.

required_system_services.description: systemd unit names the surface depends on (e.g. 'mpd.service', 'NetworkManager.service'). The admission-time parity gate verifies each is loadable.

verification.description: Read-only commands the framework runs as the surface's declared service identity to confirm the contract is satisfied. Failure = admission refusal.

commands.description: Shell commands. MUST be read-only. The framework runs each in a transient unit under the declared identity and confirms exit 0.

expected.description: Human-readable expectations the commands collectively confirm. Used in admission-failure messages and audit reports.

host_provisioning.description: Per-distribution OS-level provisioning the surface needs. Keys are distribution names (lowercase, e.g. 'debian', 'fedora', 'alpine'). Each block declares the systemd unit shape, polkit drop-in, and any additional Linux capabilities. Required for isolation='oop'; optional and advisory for isolation='in_process' (in-process privileges union into the steward record).

systemd.description: systemd unit identity + sandboxing for this surface on this distribution.

User.description: Service identity. Optional. When present: 'dynamic' selects DynamicUser=yes; a named user persists state across restarts. When absent: the installer determines per distribution policy (typically the operator's first user at uid >= 1000), and the runtime parity check resolves to whatever the deployed unit declares. Keeping User absent at framework / distribution-bootstrap level is the appliance-class default; vendor distributions that pre-bake a service user pin a name explicitly.

SupplementaryGroups.description: Supplementary groups granted to the service identity. Reference-counted at uninstall: groups shared across surviving plugins persist; groups unique to this surface are removed.

CapabilityBoundingSet.description: Linux capabilities the unit MAY receive (upper bound). Empty = no capabilities permitted.

AmbientCapabilities.description: Linux capabilities the unit starts with (subset of CapabilityBoundingSet).

ReadWritePaths.description: Filesystem paths the unit may read/write. Restrict tightly; broad paths are over-provisioning.

ReadOnlyPaths.description: Filesystem paths the unit may read but not write.

NoNewPrivileges.description: systemd NoNewPrivileges= value. Optional; default true. Setting false requires explicit justification at audit.

polkit.description: polkit drop-in granting authorisation for the surface's identity. Omit if no polkit-mediated actions are needed.

policy_file.description: Filename for the drop-in (placed in /etc/polkit-1/rules.d/ by the distribution-provided unit-installer). Convention: '50-evo-<plugin-id>.rules'.

required_actions.description: polkit action identifiers the drop-in authorises (e.g. 'org.freedesktop.NetworkManager.*'). Wildcards permitted at the trailing component.

capabilities.description: Additional Linux capabilities outside CapabilityBoundingSet (legacy / explicit grants). SHOULD be empty; non-empty entries are flagged at audit for justification.

sudoers.description: Explicit sudoers entries for this surface. SHOULD be empty for runtime plugin paths (per ADR-0087 Pillar I + ADR-0084 §4 grant-mechanism order). Non-empty entries are flagged at audit and require explicit operator/CLI-only justification.
